data.title: DeepSeek claims theoretical daily cost-profit ration of 545%
data.description:
DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) on Saturday claimed a theoretical daily cost-profit ratio of 545% related to its V3 and R1 models, but it warned that actual revenue would be much lower.

The AI startup’s post on GitLab was the first time it disclosed any profit margin information from “inference” tasks, which is the stage that involves trained AI models making predictions and doing tasks after they have been trained.

DeepSeek said in the post that, assuming the hourly rent for a H800 is $2, the total daily inference costs for its V3 and R1 models would be $87,072. It said, in contrast, theoretical daily revenue generated by the models is $562,027. That would total more than $205M revenue in a year.

H800 chips are produced by Nvidia (NVDA).

However, DeepSeek said actual revenue would be “substantially lower” because the cost of using its V3 model is less than the R1 model, just some services are monetized, and developers pay less for off-peak usage. '

DeepSeek emerged in late January when it released an AI model that reportedly outperformed Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI in many third-party tests and sparked debate about American dominance in the AI Sector.

data.title: Insurers jump, crypto-tied stocks slide in week's biggest financial movers
data.description:
Insurance companies dominated the biggest weekly winners among financial stocks, while crypto-linked firms led the steepest declines overall.

For the week ended Feb. 28, the Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) drove up 2.8%, compared with the S&P 500's 1.0% dip. In addition to the flurry of corporate earnings reports, much of this week's price action centered on uncertainties stemming from Trump's tariff moves and hot inflation data.

Root (ROOT) was the week's biggest gainer of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B), surging 35%, after the auto insurance platform achieved its first-ever net income profitability;

Real estate investment manager Bridge Investment Group Holdings (BRDG) gapped up 33% on the back of an agreement to sell itself to Apollo Global Management for $1.5B;

Goosehead Insurance (GSHD), which during the week posted better-than-expected Q4 headline results, advanced 16%;

Annuities provider Jackson Financial (JXN) gained 12%; and

Erie Indemnity Company (ERIE) rounded out the five biggest financial winners, accelerating 12%, after posting mixed Q4 results.

Conversely, crypto miners Cleanspark (CLSK) and Riot Platforms (RIOT) fell the most of any financial stock, sliding 14% and 11%, respectively, as the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) dropped double-digits on the week;

Taking the third slot, Hong Kong-based Futu Holdings (FUTU) retreated 10%;

Coinbase Global (COIN) shed 8.4% even after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday dismissed its civil 2023 lawsuit against the crypto exchange;

AI-powered lending platform Upstart Holdings (UPST) dipped 7.1%.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Nike, Lucid and Block stocks among top picks
data.description:
Major U.S. indexes closed higher on Friday, after the week saw headlines including semiconductor giant Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings report, more tariff-related moves and a heated argument between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) declined nearly 4%, while Dow (DJI) fell 0.5%.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Jefferies upgrades Nike, says ‘now is the right time’ to buy shares

Jefferies upgraded Nike (NYSE:NKE) to a Buy from Hold, with analyst Randal Konik saying that now is the right time to aggressively buy shares of the sportswear giant.

“Nike’s brand ubiquity and global distribution advantage should allow it to outgrow the market – we project a ~7% CAGR vs ~3% for the street,” Konik said and hiked PT for the stock by 53%.

Konik further added that he expects a V-shaped margin/EPS recovery in FY27 well ahead of the consensus estimates.

Conagra downgraded at Goldman Sachs on supply constraints

Goldman Sachs downgraded Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) to Neutral from Buy on what analysts believe is an elongated path of improvement for Conagra, with less visibility.

The brokerage, which set a PT of $26, noted certain continued headwinds tied to increasing competition, higher promotions, and rising input costs. GS also believes recent supply constraint issues delay the sales improvement story for Conagra Brands.

Twilio upgraded on improved execution

Morgan Stanley upgraded Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) to Overweight from Equal-Weight, citing improving execution.

“We are increasingly confident in crediting TWLO's execution toward double-digit growth and operating margin outperformance on back of increased cross-sell, supported by our recent checks,” analyst Meta Marshall and upped PT to $160 from $144.

Block receives upgrades from MS, BMO

Block (NYSE:XYZ) was in focus after the fintech stock was upgraded by Morgan Stanley and BMO.

BMO Capital Markets upgraded the stock to Outperform from Market Perform as analyst Rufus Hone sees the potential for investor sentiment to improve through 2025 as Cash App/Square gross profit growth accelerates.

Similarly, Morgan Stanley upgraded it to Equalweight from Underweight and see the risk/reward for XYZ as more balanced.

Lucid downgraded after quarterly results, CEO transition

Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) was downgraded to Under Perform by BofA after the company issued its Q4 earnings report and announced a CEO transition.

The brokerage thinks the departure of Lucid founder Peter Rawlinson is much more consequential than perhaps understood by the market.

"We note that LCID in prior public filings stated that Rawlinson was a key member, andthat it is highly dependent on the services of Peter Rawlinson, who is a significantinfluence on and driver of our technology development and business plan."

BofA believes risks are piling up for Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) and downgraded the stock to Underperform from Neutral. The brokerage cut 23% off its PT down to $10 and said one of the more significant risks to Rivian is increased competition as new SUVs/and crossovers enter the EV market next year and into 2027 from Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID), Chevy (GM), and Ford (F).

Chinese e-commerce and cloud service giant Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) was included in Benchmark's Best Ideas list. The firm noted that as AI adoption increases in China, driven by the breakout of DeepSeek, BABA is best positioned to capitalize on the growth.

Morgan Stanley upgraded Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU) to Overweight from Equalweight, as the company's momentum in the small business sector builds, its efficiency initiatives start yielding, and its conservative H2 2025 outlook bodes well for positive earnings revisions in the near term.

data.title: Bulls vs. Bears: Nvidia Q4 results divide SA community
data.description:
Nvidia Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA) was the subject of a wide range of ratings from Seeking Alpha analysts as shares of the semiconductor giant fell this week, even after its Q4 2024 financials and full-year outlook released on Wednesday exceeded Street forecasts.

Here are some excerpts from the most bullish and bearish Seeking Alpha articles on the Jensen Huang-led company published after the company’s latest earnings report.

The Bulls

“Nvidia delivered 77.9% revenue growth in Q4, with 93.4% growth in the data center market,” wrote SA analyst Hunter Wolf Research in “Nvidia Q4: Blackwell Production Ramp-Up Strong.” “Nvidia’s data center business has outperformed AMD (AMD) and Intel (INTC) in recent years. It is evident that Intel is struggling for growth, and AMD is playing a catch-up, but far behind.”

“Nvidia's data center sales surged 94% YOY, and the company consistently beats EPS estimates, making its current valuation attractive for a market leader,” added SA Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov in “Nvidia: The King Of AI Is Still A Strong Buy.” “Nvidia Corporation's strong financial performance, including top and bottom-line beats and better-than-expected guidance, continues to support a bullish outlook despite market skepticism.”

“Nvidia projects $5 billion in automotive AI revenue this fiscal year, nearly tripling last year’s sales,” noted SA analyst Deep Value Investing in “Nvidia: The Shift Towards Reasoning Models And The Importance Of Hedging.” “I believe Nvidia Corporation’s record Q4 data center revenue and Blackwell AI's rapid adoption confirm the high demand for AI infrastructure.”

The Bears

“Despite strong earnings, Nvidia's guidance indicates slower revenue growth and increasing operating expenses, making NVDA stock a poor long-term investment,” cautioned SA Investing Group Leader The Value Portfolio in “Nvidia And The Law Of Large Numbers.” “Major competitors like Apple, Intel, and Amazon are ramping up efforts, posing significant threats to Nvidia's market share in the long term.”

“Despite impressive Q4 results, Nvidia's gross margins fell below expectations, and management anticipates further declines due to the Blackwell architecture ramp-up,” opined analyst Noah's Arc Capital Management in “Nvidia Q4 Earnings Review: Losing On AI Inference.” “Nvidia's valuation remains high compared to sector medians, and shrinking margins could lead to P/E multiple compression, presenting downside risks.”

data.title: Super Micro just regained Nasdaq compliance: What’s ahead for the volatile stock?
data.description:
Shares of Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) climbed ~12% on Wednesday after the company filed its long-overdue financial reports with the U.S. SEC at the eleventh hour, avoiding a possible delisting from Nasdaq and removing a key overhang for its stock.

However, for the week, shares of the storage, server, and networking provider closed sharply lower, indicating that investors are not yet fully onboard with its outlook after a year of selloff driven by everything from governance issues to regulatory scrutiny.

Attributing SMCI’s mid-week spike to retail investors cheering the long-awaited SEC filings Seeking Alpha analyst Brett Ashcroft Green assumed that institutional investors caused the subsequent selloff given the new risk items the company mentioned in its latest 10-K filing.

“I think the additional notes will keep institutional money scared for a while,” Brett Ashcroft Green wrote, reaffirming his Hold rating on the stock.

Meanwhile, SA analyst Livy Investment Research upgraded Super Micro (NASDAQ:SMCI) to Sell from Hold and argued that the company’s decision to submit SEC filings at long last represents "a step in the right direction.”

“However, it’s likely that SMCI’s ongoing troubles are still far from being over,” the analyst added, pointing to the San Jose, California-based firm’s “unresolved ongoing accounting woes—which is the root cause that initiated the filing delays.”

Arguing that the SMCI’s new independent auditor BDO USA has not flagged any new accounting concerns per the company’s 10-K, SA analyst DeVas Research issued a Strong Buy on SMCI. “I don't think the market has fully priced in the implications of the timely filings,” DeVas Research wrote. The analyst also noted that Super Micro (NASDAQ:SMCI) stock remains undervalued ahead of a potentially significant growth trajectory in AI data center builds.

SA analyst RI Research agreed with a Strong Buy rating. “Reputable auditors from BDO confirmed that SMCI's FY2024 financials are free from material error, which confirms the company's stellar growth trajectory,” RI Research wrote, adding that the stock’s valuation “is extremely attractive with a significant upside potential.”

data.title: Data center AI growth drives up networking market demand: Jefferies
data.description:
Significant growth is expected in data centers, driving up networking demand as artificial intelligence continues to push for faster interconnect speeds, according to Jefferies.

This has prompted the investment firm to initiate coverage of both Coherent (NYSE:COHR) and Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) with Buy ratings.

"The bull thesis for COHR is not some flashy technology transition driving share, in fact it is quite the opposite," said Jefferies analysts, led by Blayne Curtis, in an investor note. "COHR is dominant in the current transceiver market at 400G (and should be at 800G), but has been dragged down by a litany of failed acquisitions, restructuring, and unnecessary spending across the board."

"The concerns about the ability for VCSELs to remain viable at 200G/Lane will be a fear, but the company remains confident in their ability to produce a 200G VCSEL product and has a material and growing EML/SiPho business as well," Curtis added.

However, Jefferies lowered its price target to $110 from $135 on Coherent.

Coherent reported record revenue of $1.43B, which was a 27% year-over-year increase, for the second quarter of fiscal 2025.

"This was driven by growth in multiple areas, including strong AI-related datacom transceiver growth, a second quarter of sequential growth in our telecom revenue, and sequential growth across multiple key industrial end markets," said Coherent CEO Jim Anderson, in the earnings call.

Meanwhile, Jefferies lowered its price target on Lumentum to $100 from $110.

"In stark contrast to COHR, LITE is a technology transition story," Cuurtis noted. "As lane speeds move to 200G/Lane (necessary for 1.6T), current VCSEL solutions have proven to be ineffective. The primary option thus far has been EML, where LITE is one of just three laser producers. The EML laser market is sold out through the end of the year, leading to strong growth and positive pricing dynamics. While LITE has historically been unsuccessful in competing on the VCSEL side, the move to EMLs brings the market to their expertise."

data.title: TeraWulf targets 200-250 MW HPC capacity by 2026 while securing $1B Core42 contract
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TeraWulf Inc. (NASDAQ:WULF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Paul Prager stated that 2024 was a transformational year for TeraWulf, highlighting the doubling of revenue and adjusted EBITDA year-over-year, driven by higher Bitcoin production and favorable pricing. He emphasized the company’s strategic shift towards high-performance computing (HPC) hosting with the execution of a 10-year, 72.5 MW data center lease agreement with Core42, valued at over $1 billion in revenue.
  • The company achieved a hash rate of 9.7 exahash per second by year-end 2024 and expects to increase this to 13.1 exahash per second upon full deployment of new S21 Pro miners.
  • CFO Patrick Fleury detailed financial improvements, noting a 29% quarter-over-quarter growth in GAAP revenue to $35 million in Q4. He also mentioned a 24% decrease in self-mined Bitcoin volume due to planned outages and miner refresh programs. The average value per Bitcoin mined increased significantly to $82,739 from $61,075 in Q3.
  • Other key developments included the restructuring of the Lake Mariner ground lease, securing infrastructure capacity for up to 750 MW, and the initiation of the Cayuga site, which is expected to add 150 MW of capacity by 2026.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its focus on scaling HPC hosting, aiming to contract and deploy 100 to 150 MW of capacity annually over the next three years. Paul Prager outlined plans to exit 2026 with 200 to 250 MW of revenue-generating HPC capacity.
  • The company expects significant revenue growth from its initial Core42 agreement, which will generate $100 million annually over the 10-year term. Additional contracts for HPC hosting could add $140 million to $210 million in annual revenue.
  • Discussions with Core42 regarding additional power options, exercisable through March 31, 2025, are ongoing. Management anticipates providing detailed 2025 guidance soon.

Financial Results

  • GAAP revenue for Q4 2024 was $35 million, a 29% increase from $27.1 million in Q3. Year-over-year, GAAP revenue rose by 102% to $140.1 million.
  • Operating expenses increased 69% quarter-over-quarter to $2.7 million, driven by staffing expansions for mining and HPC activities. SG&A expenses rose significantly to $32.3 million in Q4, primarily due to stock-based compensation.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $2.5 million, down from $6.3 million in Q3, while annual adjusted EBITDA more than doubled year-over-year to $60.4 million.
  • The company ended 2024 with $274 million in cash and total assets of $787 million.

Q&A

  • Nick Giles, B. Riley Securities, asked about the economic positioning of the Lake Mariner site and its suitability for inference workloads. Paul Prager responded that the site was fully contracted and capable of meeting both training and inference demands.
  • Mike Grondahl, Northland Securities, inquired about marketing efforts for the remaining 250 MW at Lake Mariner. Prager confirmed discussions with potential customers for long-term scalable needs.
  • Darren Aftahi, ROTH Capital, sought updates on the Core42 agreement and contingency plans if additional capacity is not exercised. Prager noted ongoing dialogues with other high-quality counterparties.
  • Kevin Cassidy, Rosenblatt Securities, questioned the impact of Bitcoin price declines on mining operations and the deployment timeline for new miners. Prager stated that the company’s focus remains on transitioning to HPC hosting rather than Bitcoin mining.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the progress in HPC hosting and the scalability of the Lake Mariner and Cayuga sites. Questions highlighted concerns about power costs, customer contracts, and market demand.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strategic shifts and scalability advantages. During the Q&A, Paul Prager frequently highlighted the company's readiness to meet customer demands and its long-term vision for HPC hosting.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone of analysts was more focused on contract execution and market positioning, while management demonstrated increased confidence in its transition strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize HPC hosting, with specific targets for annual capacity deployment and revenue growth. The previous quarter focused more on Bitcoin mining efficiency and financial realignment.
  • Management confidence increased, driven by the Core42 agreement and other strategic advances. Analysts’ tone reflected heightened interest in the company’s ability to execute on its HPC ambitions.
  • Financial metrics showed improved revenue and adjusted EBITDA year-over-year. Operating expenses and SG&A costs rose significantly, reflecting investments in scaling operations.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenges such as elevated power prices and record network difficulty impacting Bitcoin mining operations. Mitigation strategies include transitioning to HPC hosting and optimizing fleet efficiency.
  • Analysts raised concerns about dependency on Core42 for initial HPC hosting revenue and the timeline for additional site integrations, such as Cayuga.
  • The company’s ability to secure additional customer contracts and project financing remains a critical factor for future growth.

Final Takeaway

TeraWulf’s Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted its strategic pivot towards high-performance computing hosting, with a focus on scaling operations at Lake Mariner and integrating the Cayuga site. The $1 billion Core42 agreement underscores the company’s ability to attract high-quality customers, while plans to contract additional HPC capacity position it for long-term growth. However, rising operating costs and the dependency on contract execution remain key areas for investor attention.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Exxon-led consortium has recovered $33.9B in Guyana, VP says
data.description:
The consortium led by Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) in Guyana had recovered $33.9B by the end of January of the $41.1B it has spent at the Stabroek offshore block, the country's Vice President, Bharrat Jagdeo, told Reuters on Friday.

The Exxon-led (NYSE:XOM) group can take and export as much as 75% of the crude it produces as "cost oil," according to the cost recovery mechanism included in its production sharing agreement; Guyana's government is entitled to half of the remaining barrels.

The figure explained by Jagdeo includes the consortium's spending to explore more than 30 wells at the block, and the development of six oil and gas projects approved by the government.

As the group progresses towards the approval and development of new projects - it is currently planning its eighth project, Longtail - more costs will be added to the entire block, but the companies also are recovering at a faster rate every year due to the velocity of the production increase, according to the report.

data.title: Crude oil edges lower, with U.S.-Ukraine tensions latest addition to uncertainty
data.description:
Oil futures finished the week with modest losses, weighed by uncertainty around the economic impact of imminent U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico along with an extra 10% duty on Chinese imports, the likelihood of OPEC and its allies keeping output cuts in place beyond March, talks aimed at ending the war in Ukraine, and the potential resumption of oil flows from Iraq's Kurdistan region.

On the bullish side, crude prices were supported by an unexpected drop in U.S. crude inventories, new sanctions on Iran's oil trading, and President Trump's decision to revoke the license that allows Chevron to produce oil in Venezuela.

Traders are reducing risks due to rising volatility as Trump steps up his tariffs plan, not least against China, significantly raising concerns about global demand, according to Saxo Bank head of commodity strategy Ole Hansen.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for April delivery closed -0.9% this week to $69.76/bbl, the front-month's sixth straight weekly decline, and front-month April Brent crude (CO1:COM) ended -1.7% for the week to $73.18/bbl; on Friday, WTI fell 0.8% and Brent dropped 1.1%.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) finished the week -7.1% at $3.834/MMBtu, as warmer weather forecasts for early March are expected to slow the pace of inventory withdrawals in the final stretch of winter; on Friday, the front-month contract fell 2.5%.

For the month of February, WTI crude lost 3.8% and Brent lost 4.6%, while natural gas surged 26% as the cold winter accelerated the bullish narrative.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Crude oil futures were strengthening late in the session until the televised argument in the Oval Office President Trump and Ukraine's President Zelenskiy over a possible ceasefire agreement in the Russia-Ukraine war, and the meeting ended early without the expected signing of an agreement for joint development by the two countries of Ukraine's mineral resources.

"This translates to a favorable posture for Russia and the potential for them to get more oil on the market," Again Capital's John Kilduff said.

The tense meeting "creates more uncertainty [in terms of] where we go from here," Tariq Zahir of Tyche Capital Advisors said, although Trump's subsequent post on Truth Social, in which he said Zelensky can "come back when he is ready for Peace," at least leaves an opening for a potential deal.

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), finished roughly flat for the week.

Top 5 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Oil States International (OIS) +14.7%, AES Corp. (AES) +9.4%, Helix Energy Solutions (HLX) +9.2%, Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) +9.1%, Primoris Services (PRIM) +8.6%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Ameresco (AMRC) -36.8%, Shoals Technologies (SHLS) -32.9%, Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE) -27.1%, Civitas Resources (CIVI) -21.8%, Array Technologies (ARRY) -19.9%, Gran Tierra Energy (GTE) -18.8%, Montauk Renewables (MNTK) -18.5%, Eco Wave Power (WAVE) -18.5%, FuelCell Energy (FCEL) -18.4%, Sempra (SRE) -17.8%.

Source: Barchart.com
